tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows significantly elevated creatinine and proteinuria, suggesting impaired renal function, possibly chronic kidney disease. Hyperkalemia is present. Urine is abnormal in appearance and color, with glucosuria and ketonuria despite normal blood glucose and low HbA1c, which may indicate tubular dysfunction rather than diabetes. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended to clarify the underlying cause and assess for possible renal tubular disorders.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
